concerns.text: The plan excludes subjects with mean FD > 0.2mm (FR-006) but does not explicitly model residual motion effects in the regression. In fMRI studies, even sub-threshold motion correlates with entropy and behavior. Omitting FD as a covariate in the final OLS model (despite listing it in the data model) risks confounding the entropy-creativity association with residual motion artifacts.

The plan uses OLS with robust SEs for cross-sectional data but fails to address the non-independence of observations within the 7 canonical networks. Testing 7 networks without a multivariate approach (e.g., MANOVA) or a hierarchical model structure ignores the covariance structure of the data, potentially inflating Type I error rates even with BH-FDR correction.

The sensitivity analysis sweeps the entropy tolerance parameter `r` (0.15, 0.20, 0.25) but does not include a sweep for the embedding dimension `m` (default 2). Sample Entropy is highly sensitive to `m`; fixing `m=2` without sensitivity testing introduces a potential parameter artifact that could invalidate the robustness claim.

The plan assumes ~1000 subjects provide sufficient power for 7 network tests + covariates but provides no power analysis. With an expected small-to-moderate effect size (r ~ 0.1-0.2) common in neuroimaging-behavior links, the effective N after motion exclusion (often 10-20% loss) may be underpowered to detect significant effects after FDR correction, leading to false negatives.
